Name: Alarm Name
Descr: Alarm Probable Cause
Remarks: Alarm Handle Measures

Name: a-poiOverallAlarm
Descr: The alarm will occur while any sub-alarm occur in A-POI module.
Remarks:
1. Check the  alarm source to find the root cause
2. Clear the related alarm to clear the overall alarm

Name: a-poiDlInputShutdownAlarm
Descr: The detected channel power >38dBm(Low Power), >50dBm(High Power)
Remarks:
1. Please check the RIU input power
2. Add the suitable attenuator on the input port of RIU

Name: a-poiFanFault
Descr: The alarm will occur while any one of FANs fail.
Remarks:
1. Check the connection of the FAN
2. Replace the related FAN module

Name: a-poiLowInputPower
Descr: The alarm will occur while DC input power<38V or AC  input power<100V.
Remarks:
1. Check the power socket connection
2. Change the PSU module for AC supply
3. Change the RIU

Name: a-poiOverTemperature
Descr: The detected temperature greater than overtemperature threshold.
Remarks:
1. Check if there is FAN alarm
2. Check the environment temperature, need to guarantee the environment temperature no more than 45 degree
3. Check the mechanical of the device, make sure there is no dust in the device
4. Keeps some clearance (20cm) of the device

Name: a-poiDlInputOverloadAlarm
Descr: The detected channel power >37dBm(Low Power), >49dBm(High Power)
Remarks:
1. Please check the RIU input power
2. Add the suitable attenuator on the input port of RIU

Name: a-poiDlRfLowPower
Descr: The detected channel power <-40dBm(default value) .
Remarks:
1. Please check the input  power
2. Adjust the suitable attenuator on the input port of RIU
3. Check the RF input connection of RIU
4. Change the RIU

Name: a-poiVersionMismatch
Descr: Module version mismatch to A-POI.
Remarks:
1. Please check the device version and system version
2. Please check the release note for the version match

Name: a-poiServiceOff
Descr: A-POI service off - A-POI cann't operation.
Remarks:
1. Check the other alarms in the device
2. Try clear the related alarm
3. Turn on the RF switch
4. Change the unit if necessary

Name: euOverallAlarm
Descr: The alarm will occur while any sub-alarm occur in the unit.
Remarks:
1. Check the  alarm source to find the root cause
2. Clear the related alarm to clear the overall alarm

Name: euInputUnderVoltage
Descr: The alarm will occur while DC input power<38V or AC  input power<100V.
Remarks:
1. Check the input power voltage range
2. Check the connection of the power socket
3. Replace the module

Name: euPowerSupplyOverTemperature
Descr: The alarm will occur while power supply in high temperature.
Remarks:
1. Check if there is FAN alarm
2. Check the environment temperature, need to guarantee the environment temperature no more than 45 degree
3. Check the mechanical of the device, make sure there is no dust in the device
4. Keeps some clearance (20cm) of the device

Name: euFanFault
Descr: The alarm will occur while any one of FANs(totally 4) fail.
Remarks:
1. Check the connection of the FAN
2. Replace the related FAN module

Name: euOverTemperature
Descr: The detected temperature greater than overtemperature threshold.
Remarks:
1. Check if there is FAN alarm
2. Check the environment temperature, need to guarantee the environment temperature no more than 45 degree
3. Check the mechanical of the device, make sure there is no dust in the device
4. Keeps some clearance (20cm) of the device

Name: euCpriLinkTxFault
Descr:
Indicates one of the following SFP problems :TX/RX levels, Temperature,
TX/RX fault, TX/RX disable, absence (removing the SFP from the cage) .
Remarks:
1. Check if a fiber optic cable is disconnected, make sure the connection is stable
2. Check if SFP problem- try to replace with another SFP
3. Clean fiber optic cable/connector

Name: euCpriLinkRxFault
Descr:
Indicates one of the following SFP problems :TX/RX levels, Temperature,
TX/RX fault, TX/RX disable, absence (removing the SFP from the cage) .
Remarks:
1. Check if a fiber optic cable is disconnected, make sure the connection is stable
2. Check if SFP problem- try to replace with another SFP
3. Clean fiber optic cable/connector

Name: euCpriSyncFault
Descr: Indicates the SFP Synthesizer failure .
Remarks:
1. Check if a fiber optic cable is disconnected 
2. Check if SFP problem- try to replace with another SFP
3. Clean fiber optic cable/connector
4. Check if the pair of SFPs are in same type
5. Check the software version and its release note for the limitation

Name: euCpriLinkFault
Descr: Indicates the SFP link failure.
Remarks:
1. Check if the fiber optic cable is disconnected 
2. Check if the Sync fault
2. Check if SFP problem - try to replace with another SFP
3. Clean fiber optic cable/connector
4. Check if the pair of SFPs are in same type
5. Reboot the device

Name: euCpriOverTemperature
Descr: Indicates the SFP is in high temperature.
Remarks:
1. Check if the device has the over temperature alarm, and deal with OverallAlarm 
2. Check the SFP install  
3. Change the SFP

Name: euCpriManufacturerInvalid
Descr: Indicates the SFP is not certificated by manufactured.
Remarks: 1. Change the SFP to the valid brand

Name: euSynthesizerUnlocked
Descr: Indicates the system Synthesizer failure .
Remarks:
1.Reset the device
2.Update the device to latest software version
3. Replace the device
4. Check the fiber connection for primary DCU
5. Check the SFP related alarm

Name: euHardwareFault
Descr: The alarm will occur while the hardware is fail
Remarks:
1. Check if any other alarms are occurred
2. Reboot the device
3. Change the device if hardware is fail

Name: euVersionMismatch
Descr: Module version not match to system version.
Remarks:
1. Please check the device version and system version
2. Please check the release note for the version match

Name: ruOverallAlarm
Descr: The alarm will occur while any sub-alarm occur in the unit.
Remarks:
1. Check the  alarm source to find the root cause
2. Clear the related alarm to clear the overall alarm

Name: ruHardwareFault
Descr: The alarm will occur while the hardware is fail.
Remarks:
1. Check if any other alarms are occurred

3. Change the device if hardware is fail

Name: ruOverTemperature
Descr: The alarm will occur while the temperature of RU is higher then threshold.
Remarks:
1. Check if there is FAN alarm(dMRU)
2. Check the environment temperature, need to guarantee the environment temperature no more than 45 degree
3. Check the mechanical of the device, make sure there is no dust in the device
4. Keeps some clearance (20cm) of the device

Name: ruUnderVoltage
Descr: The alarm will occur while DC input power<38V or AC  input power<100V.
Remarks:
1. Verify input power level
2. Check the connections of the power socket
3. Replace the device

Name: ruSfpTxFault
Descr:
Indicates one of the following SFP problems (aggregated per port):
TX levels(<-3dBm or >5dBm),Temperature,TX disable,
absence (removing the SFP from the cage) and TBD.
Add Port number as Trap parameter.
Remarks:
1. Check if a fiber optic cable is disconnected, make sure the connection is stable
2. Check if SFP problem- try to replace with another SFP
3. Clean fiber optic cable/connector

Name: ruSfpRxFault
Descr:
Indicates one of the following SFP problems (aggregated per port):
RX levels(<-10dBm ),Temperature, RX disable,
absence (removing the SFP from the cage) and TBD.
Add Port number as Trap parameter.
Remarks:
1. Check if a fiber optic cable is disconnected, make sure the connection is stable
2. Check if SFP problem- try to replace with another SFP
3. Clean fiber optic cable/connector

Name: ruSfpSyncfault
Descr: Indicates the SFP Synthesizer failure .
Remarks:
1. Check if a fiber optic cable is disconnected 
2. Check if SFP problem- try to replace with another SFP
3. Clean fiber optic cable/connector
4. Check if the pair of SFPs are in same type
5. Check the software version and its release note for the limitation

Name: ruSfpOverTemperature
Descr: Indicates the temperature of SFP is higher than threshold.
Remarks:
1. Check if the device has the over temperature alarm, and deal with OverallAlarm 
2. Check the SFP install  
3. Change the SFP

Name: ruSfpManufacturerInvalid
Descr: Indicates the SFP is not certificated by manufactured.
Remarks: 1. Change the SFP to the valid brand

Name: ruFanAlarm
Descr: The alarm will occur while any one of FANs(totally 4) fail.
Remarks:
1. Check the connection of the FAN
2. Replace the related FAN module

Name: ruOverPowerConsumption
Descr: The alarm will occur while RU is working over its design power consumption.
Remarks:
1. Check the other alarms in the device
2. Check if the output power if it is over the rated level 
3. Check the antenna port connections
4. Reboot the device

Name: ruOverflow
Descr: Indicates the uplink input  of RU is overflow.
Remarks:
1. Keep the enough distance between UE's and RU's antenna
2. Reboot the device

Name: ruLowTransmission
Descr: Indicates the downlink input is normal, but the downlink output of device is too low.
Remarks:
1. Check the other alarms in the device
2. Check if the output power is too low 
3. Reboot the device
4. Change the device

Name: ruVswrAlarm
Descr: Indicates the VSWR is largr than threshold (2.0).
Remarks:
1. Check the connection of antenna port, make sure it is connection with antenna or load
2. Check the connected antenna or load is in good condition
3. Change the antenna or load
4. Test the device VSWR 
5. Chane the device

Name: ruAntennaAlarm
Descr: Indicates the antenna port VSRW is largr than threshold(3.0).
Remarks:
1. Check the connection of antenna port, make sure it is connection with antenna or load
2. Check the connected antenna or load is in good condition
3. Change the antenna or load
4. Test the device VSWR 
5. Chane the device

Name: ruPaAlarm
Descr: The alarm will occur while PA is alarm.
Remarks:
1. Check the other alarms in the device
2. Reboot the device
3. Chane the device

Name: ruVersionMismatch
Descr: Module version not match to system version.
Remarks:
1. Please check the device version and system version
2. Please check the release note for the version match

Name: ruServiceOff
Descr: Device service off.
Remarks:
1. Check the other alarms in the device
2. Try clear the related alarm
3. Check the carrier, radio switch config
4. Reboot the device
5. Change the device if hardware issue

Name: ruMismatch
Descr: The RU and EU module type is not match, the RU work abnormal.
Remarks:
1. Check the other alarms in the device
2. Reboot the device
3. Connect the RU to right EU

Name: ruDLOutputLowAlarm
Descr: Download Output Power is  less than threshold(-99 dBm as default value)
Remarks:
1. Check the other alarms in the device
2. Check the input power in the AU
3. Reboot the device
4. Change the device

Name: ruDLOutputOverAlarm
Descr: Download Output Power is  more than threshold(48dBm as default value)
Remarks:
1. Check the other alarms in the device
2. Reboot the device
3. Set some ATT value in RU

5. Replace the device
